what_it_does: The single root stylesheet entry point for the ConceptV Design System. It contains only a documentation header plus 20 ordered @import statements that pull in the colour/type base, the tokens/* layer files, the core/containers ladder, and the controls component layer — establishing one link target for consumers.
observations: Pure aggregator: no rules of its own, only an @import manifest of 20 files.
A long header comment enumerates the import order and states 'IMPORT ORDER (matters — later files reference earlier tokens)'.
Each import is annotated with its purpose (e.g. surfaces.css = breakpoints/frames, depth.css = elevation ramp, motion.css = 'nothing teleports' language).
Ordering goes: colors_and_type.css → 17 tokens/*.css files → core/containers.css → tokens/controls.css.
Header explicitly excludes app/, ui_kits/, preview/ chrome from this entry point ('loaded by their own surfaces — NOT here').
Comment labels core/containers.css as 'the typed containment ladder (generative core)' and tokens/controls.css as 'the component layer'.
standouts: Names the dependency chain rationale in-file: later files var() earlier-defined tokens, so import order is load-bearing.
Self-documents a layered token architecture (surfaces, sizing, depth, motion, theme, density, focus, provenance, export, texture, etc.) in one readable index.
conventions: Uses @import url() with relative paths — a CSS-native single-entry composition.
Follows the design system's 'one home, referenced everywhere' idea by making styles.css the one home for the link.
Carries a heavy explanatory header, matching the repo's self-describing discipline.
concerns: @import chains serialize CSS fetches in the browser (each import blocks discovery of the next), a known performance trade-off for many sequential files.
notes: If briefing someone: this is the one CSS file a consumer links; it just imports the colour base, ~17 token layers, the containment core, and the controls layer in a strict order.
questions: Is there a build step that inlines/concatenates these imports for production, or is the @import chain shipped as-is?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — the header says it is the single entry point that imports everything in order, and the file body is exactly that ordered import list.
apparent_themes: Single source of truth / one entry point
Layered token architecture (L0 primitives → roles → component tokens)
intention_signals: Trying to give the design system one canonical link target and to encode the layer ordering so the cascade resolves correctly, while teaching the reader what each layer is for.
novelty: The unusually rich, ordered, annotated import manifest that doubles as a map of the token layer stack.
contribution: Contributes the canonical assembly order and the named token-layer taxonomy for the ConceptV design system as a single composable CSS entry point.
summary_for_embedding: Root stylesheet of the ConceptV Design System that @imports the colour base, ~17 ordered token layers, the containment core, and the controls component layer as one entry point.
intention_for_embedding: To provide one canonical, correctly-ordered CSS link target that assembles the whole design-token system for any consumer.
